Add `using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;` if not already present.

Add `[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicConstructors | DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructors)]` annotation to the generic type parameter `T` in these 3 methods:
- `Rehydrate<T>` (line 44): change `where T : notnull` to add the DAM attribute on `T`
- `RehydrateAsync<T>` (line 82): same
- `IsResource<T>` (line 96): same

The attribute goes on the type parameter itself, like:
```csharp
public static ArmOperation<T> Rehydrate<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicConstructors | DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructors)] T>(...)
```

## 2. GenericOperationSource.cs

Add `[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicConstructors | DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructors)]` to the class-level generic type parameter `T`:

```csharp
internal class GenericOperationSource<T> : IOperationSource<T>
```

To:
```csharp
internal class GenericOperationSource<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicConstructors | DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructors)] T> : IOperationSource<T>
```

## 3. Extensions/ArmClientBuilderExtensions.cs
Add `using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;` to the top.

Add `[RequiresUnreferencedCode("Binding strongly typed objects to configuration values is not supported with trimming.")]` and `[RequiresDynamicCode("Binding strongly typed objects to configuration values requires dynamic code.")]` to the `AddArmClient<TBuilder, TConfiguration>` method (the one at line 27).

Do NOT add these attributes to the first `AddArmClient<TBuilder>` method — only the second overload that calls `RegisterClientFactory<ArmClient, ArmClientOptions>(configuration)`.

Fix AOT IL2026/IL3050 warnings in these special files in the project at /tmp/sv-1f7981b02f214bc7868382c88a4cb407/before. Make ONLY the described changes.

## 1. Create ResourceManagerJsonContext.cs

Create a new file at `/tmp/sv-1f7981b02f214bc7868382c88a4cb407/before/ResourceManagerJsonContext.cs` with:

```csharp
// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.
// Licensed under the MIT License.

using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Text.Json;
using System.Text.Json.Serialization;

namespace Azure.ResourceManager
{
    [JsonSerializerContext]
    [JsonSerializable(typeof(ArmEnvironment))]
    [JsonSerializable(typeof(Dictionary<string, Dictionary<string, JsonElement>>))]
    [JsonSerializable(typeof(Dictionary<string, string>))]
    [JsonSerializable(typeof(ResponseError))]
    internal partial class ResourceManagerJsonContext : JsonSerializerContext
    {
    }
}
```

## 2. Edit ArmEnvironment.cs

In `/tmp/sv-1f7981b02f214bc7868382c88a4cb407/before/ArmEnvironment.cs`:

Replace:
```csharp
public override string ToString() => JsonSerializer.Serialize(this);
```
With:
```csharp
public override string ToString() => JsonSerializer.Serialize(this, ResourceManagerJsonContext.Default.ArmEnvironment);
```

## 3. Edit ArmClientOptions.cs

In `/tmp/sv-1f7981b02f214bc7868382c88a4cb407/before/ArmClientOptions.cs`:

Replace:
```csharp
var allProfile = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<Dictionary<string, Dictionary<string, JsonElement>>>(span);
```
With:
```csharp
var allProfile = JsonSerializer.Deserialize(span, ResourceManagerJsonContext.Default.DictionaryStringDictionaryStringJsonElement);
```

## 4. Edit ManagedServiceIdentity.Serialization.cs

In `/tmp/sv-1f7981b02f214bc7868382c88a4cb407/before/Common/Custom/Models/ManagedServiceIdentity.Serialization.cs`:

Change 1 - Line 31: Replace the ManagedServiceIdentityType serialization:
Replace:
```csharp
            JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, ManagedServiceIdentityType, jOptions);
```
With (inline the converter logic - write property name and string value):
```csharp
            writer.WritePropertyName("type"u8);
            writer.WriteStringValue(ManagedServiceIdentityType.ToString());
```

Change 2 - Line 49: Replace UserAssignedIdentity serialization:
Replace:
```csharp
                    JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, item.Value, jOptions);
```
With:
```csharp
                    writer.WriteObjectValue(item.Value, options);
```

Change 3 - Line 206: Replace ManagedServiceIdentityType deserialization:
Replace:
```csharp
                    type = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ManagedServiceIdentityType>($"{{{property}}}");
```
With (inline the converter read logic - just extract the string value):
```csharp
                    type = new ManagedServiceIdentityType(property.Value.GetString());
```

Change 4 - Line 218: Replace UserAssignedIdentity deserialization:
Replace:
```csharp
                        dictionary.Add(new ResourceIdentifier(property0.Name), JsonSerializer.Deserialize<UserAssignedIdentity>(property0.Value.GetRawText()));
```
With:
```csharp
                        dictionary.Add(new ResourceIdentifier(property0.Name), UserAssignedIdentity.DeserializeUserAssignedIdentity(property0.Value));
```

## 5. Edit the 4 LRO files that use ToObjectFromJson

These files all have the same pattern: `ModelReaderWriter.Write(rehydrationToken, ModelReaderWriterOptions.Json, AzureResourceManagerContext.Default).ToObjectFromJson<Dictionary<string, string>>()`

Replace `.ToObjectFromJson<Dictionary<string, string>>()` with a call that uses the source-gen context.

Files:
- `/tmp/sv-1f7981b02f214bc7868382c88a4cb407/before/ManagementGroup/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ManagementGroupsArmOperation.cs` (line 61)
- `/tmp/sv-1f7981b02f214bc7868382c88a4cb407/before/ManagementGroup/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ManagementGroupsArmOperationOfT.cs` (line 61)
- `/tmp/sv-1f7981b02f214bc7868382c88a4cb407/before/Resources/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ResourcesArmOperation.cs` (line 61)
- `/tmp/sv-1f7981b02f214bc7868382c88a4cb407/before/Resources/Generated/LongRunningOperation/ResourcesArmOperationOfT.cs` (line 61)

```csharp
var lroDetails = ModelReaderWriter.Write(rehydrationToken, ModelReaderWriterOptions.Json, AzureResourceManagerContext.Default).ToObjectFromJson<Dictionary<string, string>>();
```
With:

Fix AOT IL2026/IL3050 warnings in the project at /tmp/sv-1f7981b02f214bc7868382c88a4cb407/before by replacing unsafe `JsonSerializer.Serialize`/`Deserialize` calls in serialization files. Make ONLY the described changes.

## Patterns to apply:

### Pattern 1: `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, model)` for types with IJsonModel
Replace with `writer.WriteObjectValue(model, options)` where `options` is the `ModelReaderWriterOptions` parameter already in scope.

### Pattern 2: `JsonSerializer.Deserialize<T>(someString)` for local types with `DeserializeXxx(JsonElement)` methods
Replace with the static Deserialize method, passing the JsonElement directly instead of getting raw text.

### Pattern 3: `JsonSerializer.Serialize(writer, Error)` / `JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ResponseError>(...)` for ResponseError (external Azure.Core type)
- For serialization: Replace with `writer.WriteObjectValue(Error, options)`
- For deserialization: Replace with `JsonSerializer.Deserialize(property.Value.GetRawText(), ResourceManagerJsonContext.Default.ResponseError)` 
  - Add `using Azure.ResourceManager;` if not already present for the context class access

data.content: The `[JsonSerializerContext]` attribute doesn't exist — that class is just a base class, not an attribute. Let me fix this:
data.reasoningText:
The error says `JsonSerializerContext` is abstract and cannot be used as an attribute. That's correct - `JsonSerializerContext` is not an attribute. The attribute to use is `[JsonSourceGenerationOptions]` or just not using the class as an attribute. The `[JsonSerializerContext]` attribute doesn't exist - instead, you just derive from `JsonSerializerContext` and add `[JsonSerializable]` attributes. The class itself is what the source generator picks up.

Wait, actually, in .NET 9, there might be changes. Let me check... In System.Text.Json, the way source generation works is:


1. You create a partial class that derives from `JsonSerializerContext`
2. You add `[JsonSerializable(typeof(T))]` attributes to the class for each type you want to serialize
3. The source generator generates the implementation

There's no `[JsonSerializerContext]` attribute - the attributes you need are `[JsonSourceGenerationOptions]` for optional configuration and `[JsonSerializable]` for each type. I need to remove that incorrect attribute line.
